# JavaScript Coding Standards

## General Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Never add a comment: 'Generated by Copilot'.

- **JavaScript Rule** - Line column markers are set at **60**, **90**, and **120**.
  - Ideally code doesn't go past line position **60**.
  - **90** when required have to.
  - Only past **120** when it becomes unavoidable.

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Modern JavaScript: Write code targeting ES2020+ features, use modern syntax and patterns.

## Code Style and Formatting

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Prettier: Follow Prettier formatting rules as configured in the project.

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use ESLint: Follow ESLint rules as configured in `.eslintrc` files.

- **JavaScript Rule** - Semicolons Required: Always use semicolons to terminate statements.

```javascript
// Good
const name = 'John';
const age = 30;

// Bad
const name = 'John'
const age = 30
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Single Quotes: Prefer single quotes for strings unless interpolation is needed.

```javascript
// Good
const message = 'Hello world';
const template = `Hello ${name}`;

// Bad
const message = "Hello world";
```

## Variable and Function Declarations

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use `const` by Default: Use `const` for variables that won't be reassigned, `let` for variables that will be reassigned. Never use `var`.

```javascript
// Good
const config = { port: 3000 };
let counter = 0;

// Bad
var config = { port: 3000 };
var counter = 0;
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Descriptive Names: Use clear, descriptive variable and function names.

```javascript
// Good
const userGameSession = createGameSession(userId);
const isValidGuess = validateGuess(guess);

// Bad
const ugs = createGS(uid);
const valid = validate(g);
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Function Declarations vs Expressions: Prefer function declarations for named functions, arrow functions for callbacks and short functions.

```javascript
// Good - Named function
function calculateScore(guesses, timeElapsed) {
  return Math.max(0, 100 - guesses * 10 - timeElapsed);
}

// Good - Arrow function for callbacks
const filteredPlayers = players.filter(player => player.isActive);

// Good - Arrow function for short operations
const formatTime = seconds => `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}:${seconds % 60}`;
```

## Object and Array Handling

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Destructuring: Prefer destructuring for extracting values from objects and arrays.

```javascript
// Good
const { username, score, isActive } = player;
const [first, second, ...rest] = players;

// Bad
const username = player.username;
const score = player.score;
const first = players[0];
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Spread Operator: Prefer spread operator over `Object.assign()` or array methods when appropriate.

```javascript
// Good
const newPlayer = { ...existingPlayer, score: newScore };
const allPlayers = [...activePlayers, ...inactivePlayers];

// Bad
const newPlayer = Object.assign({}, existingPlayer, { score: newScore });
const allPlayers = activePlayers.concat(inactivePlayers);
```

## Async/Await and Promises

- **JavaScript Rule** - Prefer Async/Await: Use async/await over Promise chains for better readability.

```javascript
// Good
async function saveGameSession(sessionData) {
  try {
    const session = await db.gameSession.create(sessionData);
    await cache.set(`session:${session.id}`, session);
    return session;
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error('Failed to save game session:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}

// Bad
function saveGameSession(sessionData) {
  return db.gameSession.create(sessionData)
    .then(session => {
      return cache.set(`session:${session.id}`, session)
        .then(() => session);
    })
    .catch(error => {
      logger.error('Failed to save game session:', error);
      throw error;
    });
}
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Handle Errors Properly: Always handle async errors with try/catch blocks.

```javascript
// Good
async function fetchPlayerData(playerId) {
  try {
    const player = await db.player.findById(playerId);
    if (!player) {
      throw new Error(`Player ${playerId} not found`);
    }
    return player;
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error(`Error fetching player ${playerId}:`, error);
    throw error;
  }
}
```

## Error Handling

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Custom Error Classes: Create specific error classes for different error types.

```javascript
// Good
class GameError extends Error {
  constructor(message, code) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'GameError';
    this.code = code;
  }
}

class ValidationError extends GameError {
  constructor(field, value) {
    super(`Invalid ${field}: ${value}`);
    this.name = 'ValidationError';
    this.field = field;
    this.value = value;
  }
}
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Validate Input: Always validate function parameters and user input.

```javascript
// Good
function joinGame(gameId, playerId) {
  if (!gameId || typeof gameId !== 'string') {
    throw new ValidationError('gameId', gameId);
  }
  if (!playerId || typeof playerId !== 'string') {
    throw new ValidationError('playerId', playerId);
  }

  // Function logic here
}
```

## Express.js Specific Rules

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Express Router: Organize routes using Express Router for better modularity.

```javascript
// Good - routes/game.js
const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();

router.get('/games/:id', getGameById);
router.post('/games', createGame);
router.put('/games/:id/join', joinGame);

module.exports = router;
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Middleware for Common Logic: Extract common functionality into middleware.

```javascript
// Good
const authenticateUser = async (req, res, next) => {
  try {
    const token = req.headers.authorization?.split(' ')[1];
    if (!token) {
      return res.status(401).json({ error: 'No token provided' });
    }

    const user = await verifyToken(token);
    req.user = user;
    next();
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid token' });
  }
};
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Consistent Response Format: Use consistent JSON response structure.

```javascript
// Good
const sendSuccess = (res, data, message = 'Success') => {
  res.json({
    success: true,
    message,
    data
  });
};

const sendError = (res, error, statusCode = 500) => {
  res.status(statusCode).json({
    success: false,
    message: error.message,
    error: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? error.stack : undefined
  });
};
```

## Socket.IO Specific Rules

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Event Namespacing: Organize socket events with clear namespacing.

```javascript
// Good
socket.on('game:join', handleGameJoin);
socket.on('game:guess', handleGameGuess);
socket.on('game:leave', handleGameLeave);

// Bad
socket.on('join', handleGameJoin);
socket.on('guess', handleGameGuess);
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Validate Socket Data: Always validate data received from socket events.

```javascript
// Good
socket.on('game:guess', (data) => {
  try {
    const { gameId, guess } = validateGuessData(data);
    handleGameGuess(socket, gameId, guess);
  } catch (error) {
    socket.emit('error', { message: error.message });
  }
});
```

## Database and Data Access

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Connection Pooling: Always use connection pooling for database connections.

```javascript
// Good
const pool = new Pool({
  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  max: 20,
  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,
  connectionTimeoutMillis: 2000
});
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Transactions for Related Operations: Group related database operations in transactions.

```javascript
// Good
async function endGame(gameId, winnerId) {
  const client = await pool.connect();
  try {
    await client.query('BEGIN');

    await client.query(
      'UPDATE games SET status = $1, winner_id = $2, ended_at = NOW() WHERE id = $3',
      ['completed', winnerId, gameId]
    );

    await client.query(
      'UPDATE players SET games_won = games_won + 1 WHERE id = $1',
      [winnerId]
    );

    await client.query('COMMIT');
  } catch (error) {
    await client.query('ROLLBACK');
    throw error;
  } finally {
    client.release();
  }
}
```

## Testing Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Descriptive Test Names: Use clear, descriptive test names that explain what is being tested.

```javascript
// Good
describe('Game Session', () => {
  it('should create a new game session with valid players', async () => {
    // Test implementation
  });

  it('should reject game creation when player limit is exceeded', async () => {
    // Test implementation
  });
});

// Bad
describe('Game', () => {
  it('should work', () => {
    // Test implementation
  });
});
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Setup and Teardown: Use beforeEach/afterEach for test setup and cleanup.

```javascript
// Good
describe('Database Operations', () => {
  beforeEach(async () => {
    await db.migrate.latest();
    await db.seed.run();
  });

  afterEach(async () => {
    await db.migrate.rollback();
  });
});
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Mock External Dependencies: Mock external services and dependencies in tests.

```javascript
// Good
jest.mock('../services/redis', () => ({
  get: jest.fn(),
  set: jest.fn(),
  del: jest.fn()
}));
```

## Security Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Sanitize Input: Always sanitize and validate user input.

```javascript
// Good
const sanitizeGuess = (guess) => {
  if (typeof guess !== 'string') {
    throw new ValidationError('guess', 'must be a string');
  }

  const sanitized = guess.trim().toLowerCase();

  if (sanitized.length === 0) {
    throw new ValidationError('guess', 'cannot be empty');
  }

  if (!/^[a-z]+$/.test(sanitized)) {
    throw new ValidationError('guess', 'must contain only letters');
  }

  return sanitized;
};
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Environment Variables for Secrets: Never hardcode secrets or sensitive data.

```javascript
// Good
const config = {
  jwtSecret: process.env.JWT_SECRET,
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  redisUrl: process.env.REDIS_URL
};

// Bad
const config = {
  jwtSecret: 'my-secret-key',
  databaseUrl: 'postgresql://user:pass@localhost/db'
};
```

## Performance Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Caching Strategically: Cache frequently accessed data with appropriate TTL.

```javascript
// Good
const getCachedWord = async (difficulty) => {
  const cacheKey = `word:${difficulty}`;
  let word = await cache.get(cacheKey);

  if (!word) {
    word = await generateRandomWord(difficulty);
    await cache.set(cacheKey, word, 3600); // 1 hour TTL
  }

  return word;
};
```

- **JavaScript Rule** - Limit Database Queries: Use efficient queries and avoid N+1 problems.

```javascript
// Good
const getGamesWithPlayers = async () => {
  const query = `
    SELECT g.*,
           json_agg(p.*) as players
    FROM games g
    LEFT JOIN game_players gp ON g.id = gp.game_id
    LEFT JOIN players p ON gp.player_id = p.id
    GROUP BY g.id
  `;

  return await db.query(query);
};

// Bad
const getGamesWithPlayers = async () => {
  const games = await db.query('SELECT * FROM games');

  for (const game of games) {
    game.players = await db.query(
      'SELECT p.* FROM players p JOIN game_players gp ON p.id = gp.player_id WHERE gp.game_id = $1',
      [game.id]
    );
  }

  return games;
};
```

## Logging Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use Structured Logging: Use structured logging with appropriate log levels.

```javascript
// Good
logger.info('Game started', {
  gameId: game.id,
  playerCount: game.players.length,
  difficulty: game.difficulty,
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
});

logger.error('Database connection failed', {
  error: error.message,
  stack: error.stack,
  query: query,
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
});

// Bad
console.log('Game started');
console.log(error);
```

## Documentation Guidelines

- **JavaScript Rule** - Use JSDoc Comments: Document functions with JSDoc comments for complex logic.

```javascript
// Good
/**
 * Calculates the score for a completed game
 * @param {number} guesses - Number of guesses made
 * @param {number} timeElapsed - Time taken in seconds
 * @param {string} difficulty - Game difficulty level
 * @returns {number} Calculated score
 */
function calculateScore(guesses, timeElapsed, difficulty) {
  const baseScore = 1000;
  const difficultyMultiplier = getDifficultyMultiplier(difficulty);
  const timePenalty = Math.floor(timeElapsed / 10);
  const guessPenalty = guesses * 50;

  return Math.max(0, (baseScore - timePenalty - guessPenalty) * difficultyMultiplier);
}
```
